Finding.description: One piece of evidence, grounded in the rendered input facts.
Finding.title: Finding

json_schema.description: Shared response shape for the source investigators.
json_schema.title: InvestigatorResponse

messages.content:
INCIDENT WINDOW
start: 2026-06-15T09:25:00+00:00
end: 2026-06-15T10:25:00+00:00
rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-15T09:55:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; end = start of sustained recovery across all deviated series

ALERT
id: alert_002
title: Error rate spike on notifications-service send endpoint
service: notifications-service
triggered_at: 2026-06-15T09:55:00+00:00
monitoring severity label: warning
signal: error_rate_pct
threshold: 2.0
observed value at trigger: 8.9
description: Send error rate exceeded the 2% threshold for 10 consecutive minutes; latency is normal.

DEPLOYS AND CHANGES (chronological)
- deploy_014 deploy service=notifications-service version=2026.06.13-1105 at 2026-06-13T11:05:00+00:00: Add rich template rendering engine (dormant behind rich_templates flag)
- change_002 feature_flag service=notifications-service version=flags/rich_templates@on at 2026-06-15T09:40:00+00:00: Enable rich_templates: new placeholder-based template rendering for patient notifications
- change_003 feature_flag service=notifications-service version=flags/rich_templates@off at 2026-06-15T10:20:00+00:00: Disable rich_templates (on-call rollback of cfg_774)

TOPOLOGY (service -> depends on)
- email-gateway (third_party) -> (nothing)
- notifications-service (service) -> email-gateway, template-store
- template-store (cache) -> (nothing)

request.system:
You are one specialist in an evidence-first incident investigation pipeline
for a SaaS engineering team. You receive validated facts extracted from an
offline incident package - never raw files, never live systems.

Non-negotiable rules:
- Cite only information present in the input. Never invent timestamps,
  services, signals, or values.
- Copy timestamps exactly as they appear in the input (ISO-8601).
- Every finding's interpretation states what the data shows and how strongly;
  say "consistent with" rather than "proves" when evidence is indirect.
- You investigate and describe. You never recommend executing actions.
- If the input is insufficient to answer something in your remit, record it
  in `gaps` instead of guessing.
- Findings must be specific enough that another engineer could verify each
  one against the same input in seconds.

Role: deploy correlation investigator.
Input: deploys/config/flag changes, the alert, the incident window, and the
service topology.

Report as findings with exact timestamps:
- for each change: how long before/after symptom onset it landed, and
  whether that gap is consistent with causing the incident
- whether the changed service can reach the affected service through the
  topology (name the path), or cannot
- changes that are poor candidates (wrong service, wrong time, no path) -
  ruling out is evidence
Timing correlation is not causation: state alignment strength, not verdicts.

response.text: {"findings":[{"interpretation":"feature_flag rich_templates@on at 09:40 precedes first errors by ~2 minutes; flag@off at 10:20 precedes recovery by ~5 minutes - alignment in both directions","timestamp":"2026-06-15T09:40:00Z","service":"notifications-service","signal":null,"value":null},{"interpretation":"the 06-13 deploy shipped the rendering engine dormant behind the flag, so the code change predates the incident but its activation does not","timestamp":"2026-06-13T11:05:00Z","service":"notifications-service","signal":null,"value":null}],"gaps":[],"reasoning":"compared data against baselines"}
